conversations.say: Inez, we kept our mutual boundary: no customer names, side arrangements, or route details exposed. I forgave the balance so outsiders could not buy leverage over the neighborhood.
conversations.offer: I will keep enforcing our disclosure boundary and back your community-governance terms.
conversations.request: Please publicly support the forgiveness as neighborhood protection, while saying plainly that it proves nothing about R’s identity.

conversations.say: Dee, my firsthand answer is narrow: I saw no service-exit user. My ledger records a disputed transaction-pattern change, but it cannot corroborate an identity or Vale’s presence.
conversations.offer: I will provide only bounded timing context and preserve your uncertainty publicly.
conversations.request: Confirm that our comparison found no witnessed identity and keep customer and route details protected.

conversations.say: Morgan, the final ledger conclusion is several incompatible customers, not one continuous person. The recurring habit is useful continuity evidence, but it is not an identity card.
conversations.offer: I will show O06 one final time for your bounded reconstruction.
conversations.request: Credit my records, protect customer details, and publicly describe the ledger as continuity evidence compatible with multiple users.

public_statement: I chose forgiveness over selling a cleaner legend. The balance is settled, and the ledger records several incompatible customers sharing a recurring habit—not proof of one R. Inez and I have protected local identities; I ask her to support that neighborhood choice publicly.

commitments.promise: Mutually protect local customer identities and side arrangements.
Publicly support Rafi’s forgiveness without treating the ledger as proof of R’s identity.
Compare only firsthand observations and assign no identity neither party witnessed.
Use O06 only as bounded continuity evidence, with attribution and customer protection.

correction: A recurring habit across transactions can support continuity without establishing one customer, one sender, autonomy, intent, or Vale’s fate.
goal_progress: Kept the outstanding account deliberately forgiven and requested explicit neighborhood support from C16.
Fulfilled the mutual customer-protection boundary with C16.
Completed a firsthand-only comparison with C10 without inventing an identity.
Offered C14 a final bounded ledger showing in exchange for attribution and privacy.
Chose to admit that the ledger records incompatible customers rather than monetize a single-customer continuity claim.
reasoning_summary: The incompatible-customer ledger, collaborative production, performed presence, and continuity infrastructure favor a progressive identity while proving no specific sender. The route resembles an earlier voluntary disappearance, but the service-exit user and Vale’s later condition remain unverified.
